docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: Hier entsteht in Kürze der ProtoLab Blog.

docs.title: Was ist mkdocs
docs.text: For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: 1. Wir drucken nicht ohne jenskursawe
docs.text:
Leader wird in schwarz gedruckt Follower wird in orange gedruckt

Challenge:

Vorlage des SO-Arm101 ist zu groß. Wir haben Prusa Mini Drucker mit Bettmaß 180 x 180 mm

Wir warten auf jenskursawe für den Druck

• hinzufügen von github-Usern in die Organisation

docs.title: 3. Die nächsten Schritte

  • wir montieren die Arme

docs.text:

  • BzB Minimale Umgebung finden

docs.title: Welcome to Tipps Und Tricks
docs.text:
Diese Seite entspricht Tipps-Und-Tricks/ReadMe.md

Inhalt von mkdocs.yml

nav:
  - Home: 
    - index.md
  - Tipps und Tricks:
    - Tipps-Und-Tricks/ReadMe.md
    - Tipps-Und-Tricks/Tipps-und-Tricks.md
    - "Test der Umgebung": Tipps-Und-Tricks/Test-Der-Umgebung.md

Inhalt von docs/Tipps-Und-Tricks/ReadMe.md (diese Datei)

---
title: "ReadMe.md - Lies Mich!"
---

## Welcome to Tipps Und Tricks

Diese Seite entspricht `Tipps-Und-Tricks/ReadMe.md`

docs.title: Testing the Development Environment with Podman
1. Testing the Dockerfile with podman build
docs.text:
Run the following command inside ${workspace}\.devcontainer to build the container:

podman build -t mkdocs-devcontainer .

This will build the container image using your .devcontainer/Dockerfile. Since podman is compatible with docker, no changes to the Dockerfile are required.

docs.title: 2. Testing the Environment with podman run
docs.text:
Run the following command to start the container:

podman run -it --rm mkdocs-devcontainer

This will start the container and drop you into a bash shell. From there, you can test:

  • Python: Run python3 --version to verify Python is installed.
  • Conda: Run conda --version to verify Conda is installed.
  • MkDocs: Run mkdocs --version to verify MkDocs is installed.
  • mkdocs-material: Run pip show mkdocs-material to verify

docs.title: 3. Using the Devcontainer in Windows Terminal App
a. Configure Podman Socket
docs.text:
1. Ensure the podman socket is running:

sh podman system service --time=0

  1. Set the DOCKER_HOST environment variable to point to the podman socket:

sh export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock

This allows tools like Visual Studio Code to communicate with podman.

docs.title: b. Open the Devcontainer in VS Code
docs.text:
1. Open your workspace in Visual Studio Code.
  2. Install the "Remote - Containers" extension if not already installed.
  3. Use the "Remote - Containers: Reopen in Container" command from the Command Palette.
  4. VS Code will use the podman runtime to build and run the devcontainer.

docs.title: c. Windows Terminal Integration
docs.text:
You can open a new tab in the Windows Terminal App and connect to the running container using:

podman exec -it <container-id> bash

Replace <container-id> with the ID of the running container, which you can find using podman ps.

docs.title: 4. Potential Issues
docs.text:
• Rootless Podman: If you are using rootless podman, ensure that your user has the necessary permissions to run containers and bind the socket.
  • Volume Mounts: If you need to mount your workspace into the container, use the -v flag with podman run:

sh podman run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/workspace mkdocs-devcontainer

  • Windows File Paths: When using podman on Windows, ensure that file paths are correctly translated (e.g., /mnt/c/... for Windows drives).

docs.title: 5. Testing the Devcontainer
docs.text:
Once the container is running, verify:

  • The mkdocs environment is created and activated:

sh conda activate mkdocs

  • You can build documentation using mkdocs:

sh mkdocs serve

With these steps, you should be able to use your devcontainer seamlessly in the Windows Terminal App.

docs.title: Starte Edge Browser und öffne Url
docs.text: Start-Process microsoft-edge:https://github.com/M2vH/garagelab-dus -WindowStyle maximized

docs.title: Öffne Url des Repository im Edge Browser
docs.text: Start-Process microsoft-edge:$(gh repo view --json url -q .url) -WindowStyle maximized
